sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 78

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 78:1
items.html:
(to the question) The point below the Yesod
The point below the Yesod is the root of the Malchut of ZA, found beneath his six ends. From there it rose and was incorporated in the whole of the middle line of ZA, in the discernment of the Nukva in his Guf; and afterwards the separate Nukva of ZA takes all of that incorporation. (p. 775, beginning "Achoraim")
